document.title: User Manual for G1417/G1717 Series detector models

sourceSummary.oneLine: User manual for H&abyz G1417/G1717 Series wireless digital flat panel detectors covering intended use, specifications, installation, maintenance, EMC, labels, warranty, and revision history.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Use this manual to set up the G1417/G1717 Series detector and understand its functions before operating the product.
keyPoints: G1417/G1717 Series are digital flat panel detectors used for screening and diagnosis of disease or injury.
The detectors are intended for qualified or trained doctors or technicians for diagnostic radiographic exposures of skull, spinal column, chest, abdomen, extremities, and other body parts.
The detectors are not intended for mammography applications.
The equipment complies with IEC 60601-1-2 EMC standards for medical devices.
The estimated product lifetime may be up to five years under appropriate regular inspection and maintenance.

entities.name: H&abyz
entities.evidence: This user manual explains how to use the G1417/G1717 Series detector models made by H&abyz.

entities.evidence: The use of accessories and cables other than those approved and sold by H&abyz Co., Ltd. May result in increased stability of the equipment.

concepts.evidence: G1417/G1717 Series are digital flat panel detectors that is used for screening and diagnosis of disease of injury.

concepts.evidence: These detectors are not intended to be used for mammography applications.

concepts.name: Wireless communication
concepts.evidence: G1417/G1717 Series detectors support IEEE 802.11a/b/g/n/ac to acquire images without a wired connection in anytime, anywhere.

evidence.claim: The document is a user manual for G1417/G1717 Series detector models made by H&abyz.
evidence.evidenceText: This user manual explains how to use the G1417/G1717 Series detector models made by H&abyz.

evidence.claim: The manual is intended for users who set up and operate G1417/G1717 Series detector models.
evidence.evidenceText: This manual is intended for the users who set up and operate the G1417/G1717 Series detector models.

evidence.claim: G1417/G1717 Series are digital flat panel detectors for screening and diagnosis of disease or injury.
evidence.evidenceText: G1417/G1717 Series are digital flat panel detectors that is used for screening and diagnosis of disease of injury.

evidence.claim: The detectors are intended for qualified or trained doctors or technicians for diagnostic radiographic exposures of multiple body parts.
evidence.evidenceText: These detectors are intended for use by a qualified/trained doctor or technician on both adult subjects for taking diagnostic radiographic exposures of the skull, spinal column, chest, abdomen, extremities, and other body parts.

evidence.claim: The detectors are not intended for mammography applications.
evidence.evidenceText: These detectors are not intended to be used for mammography applications.

evidence.claim: Only a physician or legally certified operator should use the product.
evidence.evidenceText: Only a physician or a legally certified operator should use this product.

evidence.claim: Roentgenography, image processing, image reading, and image data storage must follow laws of the country or region where the product is used.
evidence.evidenceText: Roentgenography, image processing, image reading, and image data storage must be performed in accordance with the laws of the country or region in which the product is being used.

evidence.claim: The detector model set includes G1417CW, G1417CWP, G1417FW, G1417FWP, G1717CW, G1717CWP, G1717FW, and G1717FWP.
evidence.evidenceText: Model Name G1417CW G1417CWP G1417FW G1417FWP G1717CW G1717CWP G1717FW G1717FWP

evidence.claim: The battery pack model is MBP-3S1P3330-HAZ.
G1417/G1717 Series detectors use IEEE 802.11a/b/g/n/ac wireless standards.
The wireless communication specifications are the same for the listed G1417/G1717 Series detector variants.
evidence.evidenceText: The wireless communication specifications of the G1417/G1717 Series detectors (G1417CW, G1717CW, G1417FW, G1717FW, G1417CWP, G1717CWP, G1417FWP, G1717FWP) are the same.

evidence.claim: The detectors have IP68 protection rating.
The operating environment is 10 to 35 degrees C, 20 to 75% relative humidity, and 700 to 1060 hPa atmospheric pressure.
evidence.evidenceText: Operation Temperature 10~35℃ Humidity 20~75% H.R. Atmospheric pressure 700~1060hPa

evidence.claim: The estimated product lifetime may be up to five years with appropriate regular inspection and maintenance.
evidence.evidenceText: The estimated product lifetime may be up to five (5) years under the appropriate regular inspection and maintenance.

evidence.claim: Regular inspection should be performed at least once a year.
evidence.evidenceText: be sure to perform regular inspection at least once a year.

evidence.claim: The equipment complies with IEC 60601-1-2 EMC standards for medical devices.
evidence.evidenceText: This equipment complies with IEC 60601-1-2 EMC standards for medical devices.

evidence.claim: The wireless detector is CISPR 11 Group 1 and Class A for RF emissions.
evidence.evidenceText: RF Emissions CISPR 11 Group 1 ... RF Emissions CISPR 11 Class A

evidence.claim: The product is subject to WEEE disposal instructions in the European Union and EEA.
evidence.evidenceText: European Union (and EEA*) only This symbol indicates that this product is not to be disposed with your household waste, according to the WEEE Directive (2012/19/EC) and your national law.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority signal MFDS/domestic appears in source metadata, but MFDS is not stated in the document body.
The source path includes User Manual_250429.docx, but the document body only states revision date 2024-07-22; no separate document date for 2025-04-29 is stated.
Some extracted text appears to contain OCR or conversion errors, including duplicated or inconsistent numbering and phrases such as '820.11ac' and 'Stating up'.
Product signal is provided as Unclassified in metadata, while the document body identifies G1417/G1717 Series detector models.
